title: Update grpc dependency to fix CVE-2026-33186
body:
current skopeo version (1.20-1.22) has a dependency on grpc lib that has a critical vulnerability as defined here -- https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p77j-4mvh-x3m3

Can we update the dependency to latest version (1.80.0) from (1.79.2) and make a new release?

Thanks

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

The server side of that code is never actually used in Skopeo.

Thanks @mtrmac for confirming this.
However, our CI/CD fails as vulnerability checker blocks the lib to be deployed.
Would it be OK, if I create a pull-request with the update? Thanks.

If you want to just update the dependency on the `main` branch now, that would be welcome.

As for tagging a release, I think that would be fine on `main`; I’m not sure about any implications on a stable branch. @TomSweeneyRedHat ?
… actually I see #2842 is happening already.

@prateekguru I'm not 100% sure which version of Skopeo you'd like the fix in.  But it's now in main, and v1.22.1, which originated from the Skopeo release-1.22 branch.  The main branch is headed towards v1.23.0.  The current plans for that is May/June, but no definitive time on that one, though, we're still heavily in planning mode.
Thank you @TomSweeneyRedHat ... we should be able to use 1.22.1 for our purposes.
